title: Prim, Kruskal, Wilson, Bias, and Distribution Claims · Behavior Case: Bias Metrics
summary: Algorithms are judged from one attractive screenshot, “random” is called uniform, and difficulty is inferred from generation time. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bias metrics increment in comparative seeded generation laboratory. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure corridor length, turns, dead ends, path stretch, branching, and distributions across many recorded seeds.
Construct a uniform spanning tree with loop-erased random walks and state termination assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-bias-metrics — Measure corridor length, turns, dead ends, path stretch, branching, and distributions across many recorded seeds.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-generation-alternatives-bias-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-generation-alternatives-bias-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_generation_alternatives_bias_5(seeds: int = 64, valid_trees: int = 64, algorithms: int = 4, measured_metrics: int = 5, uniformity_claim_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if seeds < 16 or valid_trees < 0:
        return False, valid_trees, "sample-budget"
    if valid_trees != seeds:
        return False, valid_trees, "invalid-generated-tree"
    if algorithms < 4 or measured_metrics < 4:
        return False, algorithms, "comparison-coverage"
    if not uniformity_claim_bounded:
        return False, measured_metrics, "distribution-overclaim"
    assert valid_trees == seeds
    return True, valid_trees, "bounded-generator-comparison"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure corridor length, turns, dead ends, path stretch, branching, and distributions across many recorded seeds.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bias Metrics model · Theory Bias Metrics, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Bias Metrics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Bias Metrics model · Theory Bias Metrics, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3627 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare multiple generators across seeded topology and bias evidence without unsupported distribution claims; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.
